data.title: Bank stocks mixed as uncertain outlook overshadows Q1 earnings
data.description:
The stocks of money center banks are mixed in early afternoon trading on Friday as heightened uncertainty makes it difficult for management to give shareholders clear direction on where the business is headed for the rest of the year.

The KBW Nasdaq Bank Index (BKX) slipped 0.5%, while the KBW Nasdaq Regional Banking Index dipped 1.1%.

JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) stock rose a solid 3.5% as it reaffirmed its 2025 guidance for net interest income, excluding markets, and delivered beats on both Q1 earnings and revenue. It's also seen as the strongest bank to weather any potential downturn.

Its capitalization remains strong with a standardized common equity tier 1 ratio of 15.4%, said Jonathan Weber, Investing Group Leader for Cash Flow Club. "To me, this suggests that risks are relatively low at JPM thanks to its strong capital levels, while a surplus of capital also means that JPMorgan could likely ramp up shareholder returns if it wants to," he said.

In JPMorgan Chase's (NYSE:JPM) earnings call, Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon expects the uncertainty resulting from the tariff drama and other government policies to spur many companies to be cautious on guidance. "My guess is, a lot will remove it," he told investors. "They're going to tell you what they think it might do to their customers, their base, their earnings, their cost, their tariffs. It's different for every company."

From his observation, "a lot of people are not doing things because of this. They're going to wait and see," he said. That applies to M&A and hiring plans, as they adjust to a new environment.

Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) stock rose 0.4%, after its Q1 earnings and revenue also beat consensus estimates on strong equity revenue and healthy client assets, even as it increased its provision for loan losses.

Trust bank BNY (NYSE:BK) also turned in a double-beat for the quarter. Its stock gained 1.4%.

In regional banks, there were few gainers. U.S. Bancorp (USB) edged up 0.1% and Bank OZK (OZK) increased 0.3%. However, PNC Financial (PNC) fell 0.6%, Flagstar Financial (FLG) -2.6%, KeyCorp (KEY) -1.5%, Truist Financial (TFC) -1.8%, and Regions Financial (RF) -1.6%.

However, Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) stock dropped 1.5% after the bank turned in a mixed quarter, with earnings topping the average analyst estimate, but revenue falling short.  Steve Booyens of Pearl Gray Equity and Research said he sees a pivot, "where asset-liability duration management will be challenging; additionally, our allowance and provision outlook is dim."

Other U.S. banks that have yet to report remained in the red. Citigroup (NYSE:C) slipped 1.2%, Goldman Sachs (GS) edged down 0.3%, Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) fell 0.7%.

For a more bullish view on banks, look north of the U.S. border. Toronto Dominion Bank (TD) stock gained 2.5%, Royal Bank of Canada (RY) rose 2.4%, and Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (CM) gained 1.4%.

European and UK bank stocks also fared better than their U.S. counterparts. Deutsche Bank (DB) climbed 3.0%, Banco Santander (SAN) +2.0%, HSBC Holdings (HSBC) +2.5%, Barclays (BCS) +1.3%, and UBS Group (UBS) +2.5%.

data.title: Fastenal outlines expanded FMI footprint and pricing adjustments for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Fastenal Company (FAST) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Dan Florness highlighted a 3.5% sales growth during Q1, with daily growth at 5%, despite ongoing sluggish market conditions. He attributed this growth to internal execution, including new customer relationships and the expansion of existing ones. Leadership changes made over the past two years are credited with driving strategic execution and improving results.
  • The company expanded its FMI technology footprint, deploying approximately 130,000 devices across 25 countries, reflecting a 12.5% growth in device count. Safety sales saw a nearly 10% increase in March, supported by this execution.
  • Digital engagement now accounts for 61% of total sales, with a target of reaching 66%-68% by October 2025. The company also reported higher success in its customer site programs, with those generating over $10,000 per month growing by 7%. Efforts are ongoing to improve e-commerce performance.
  • CFO Holden Lewis, in his final earnings call, reported Q1 sales growth of 3.4% and daily sales growth of 5%. He emphasized improved contract signings and favorable trends in national and regional agreements, which have grown double-digits for 12 consecutive months.

Outlook

  • Management implemented pricing adjustments in April 2025 to address tariff impacts, expecting these measures to contribute 3%-4% price uplift in Q2. The potential exists for this uplift to double in the second half of the year, depending on execution.
  • CEO Florness mentioned ongoing supply chain adjustments, including diversifying sourcing locations and increasing inventory to mitigate tariff risks and improve customer support.
  • The company maintains a target of advancing its digital footprint sales to 66%-68% by October 2025, supported by improvements in inventory stocking and e-commerce capabilities.

Financial Results

  • Q1 gross margin was 45.1%, down 40 basis points year-over-year, influenced by higher transportation costs and product mix. Operating margin stood at 20.1%, a decline of 50 basis points, mostly due to one less selling day in the quarter.
  • EPS for Q1 was reported at $0.52, consistent with the same period last year. Operating cash flow reached $262 million, equating to 88% of net income. Inventory levels grew by 11.9%, reflecting strategic moves to improve product availability and navigate tariff uncertainties.
  • Capital expenditures for the quarter were $53.8 million, with full-year spending projected at $265 million to $285 million. Investments include FMI devices, IT projects, and distribution center expansions.

Q&A

  • David Manthey, Baird: Questioned the company’s ability to adjust pricing in response to tariffs. CEO Florness confirmed that contracts allow for price adjustments and highlighted the company’s enhanced sourcing capabilities to mitigate tariff impacts.
  • Stephen Volkmann, Jefferies: Inquired about smoothing tariff impacts for customers. Management explained efforts to align pricing and costing through inventory planning and close communication with customers.
  • Ryan Cooke, Wolfe Research: Asked about SG&A trends and expectations for cost leveraging. Management noted stable cost controls and the potential for SG&A leverage with mid-single-digit sales growth.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about tariff impacts and sought clarity on mitigation strategies. Questions often reflected cautious optimism regarding the company’s ability to navigate cost pressures and maintain customer relationships.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing the strength of its supply chain and customer engagement strategies. Defensive language was minimal, with a focus on proactive measures and long-term growth capabilities.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, both analysts and management displayed slightly more optimism, driven by improved sales growth and internal execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 2025 reported 3.5% sales growth compared to 3.7% in Q4 2024. Daily sales growth improved from 2.1% in Q4 2024 to 5%. Gross margin pressures eased slightly, with Q1 2025 gross margin at 45.1% versus Q4 2024’s 44.8%.
  • Management’s tone showed increased confidence in internal execution capabilities, while analysts focused on the implications of tariffs and e-commerce challenges.
  • Strategic priorities shifted towards enhancing e-commerce capabilities and mitigating tariff-related risks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariffs on steel-based and non-steel products remain a significant challenge. Management is addressing these risks through pricing actions and sourcing diversification.
  • E-commerce underperformance continues to be a concern, with efforts underway to improve customer experience and inventory visibility.
  • Higher inventory levels and capital expenditures could weigh on cash flow if sales momentum slows.

Final Takeaway

Fastenal’s Q1 2025 performance reflects strong internal execution amid sluggish market conditions. Management’s focus on expanding FMI technology, digital sales, and strategic sourcing adjustments positions the company to navigate tariff challenges and capture growth opportunities. Pricing actions initiated in Q2 and ongoing enhancements in e-commerce and supply chain capabilities are expected to drive performance in the coming quarters.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Biotech stocks to own amid market volatility – Piper Sandler
data.description:
Piper Sandler analysts highlighted their top picks for biotechnology for investors amid the rollercoaster seen in the equity markets.

“Trump's tariffs/Trade War has ignited uncertainty, resulting in roller-coaster volatility across markets,” said Edward A. Tenthoff, senior research analyst at Piper Sandler. “While macro headlines/tweets continue to dictate stock performance, and we expect volatility to continue, we see biotech (NYSEARCA:XBI) as positioned for recovery.”

The sector is defensive, and biopharmaceuticals have, for the most part, limited tariff and transfer price exposure, he explained. Also, the biotech sector is highly susceptible to capital costs and interest rates, and Piper Sandler’s macro team forecasts four rate cuts over the next year, starting in June.

Tenthoff also mentioned not expecting major FDA delays and greater innovation in the sector as tailwinds for it. “The sector is delivering more lifesaving therapies and even cures every year.”

Here are the names to own, according to Piper Sandler:

Alnylam Pharmaceuticals (ALNY) -0.56% YTD

“Alnylam guided TTR revenue growth of 31-41% to $1.6-1.725 billion in 2025, which we project could further grow to $2.75 billion in 2026.”

argenx SE (ARGX) -5.68% YTD

“As the company's innovation strategy beyond MG and CIDP comes into focus, with a win in myositis a nice recent example and our interest piqued in next-gen FcRn, ARGX-213, we look for increasing pipeline value to work its way into the stock.”

Axsome Therapeutics (AXSM) 14.73% YTD

“We think the shares are quite inexpensive as an emerging profitability story trading at an EV/2026E EBITDA of ~26x in the context of a multi-product/opportunity CNS-story that has high visibility into a transformative longer-term EBITDA CAGR (well in excess of 40% in our view (2026+)).”

BridgeBio Pharma (BBIO) 22.56% YTD

“BBIO looks attractive during this market volatility time as it has had an initial strong launch of Attruby, a treatment for ATTR-CM, and we think Q1 company-compiled consensus estimates of $13.5M are conservative.”

Legend Biotech Corp. (LEGN) -1.66% YTD

“Despite being a Chinese company, CARVYKTI is manufactured and IP is domiciled in the U.S., so Legend has no material tariff or transfer price exposure. Legend ended 2024 with cash of ~$1.1 billion, expected to fund the company to operating profit in 2Q:26.”

Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL) 0.74% YTD

“We continue to be bullish on MDGL's REZDIFFRA (resmetirom) launch where consensus (per FactSet) estimates for 1Q25 and 2025 are $113.7M (we model ~$112.6M) and $577.3M (we model ~$535.7M), respectively.”

Neurocrine Biosciences (NBIX) -33.89% YTD

“NBIX is one of our highest conviction larger cap ideas… [due to] high visibility into continued meaningful Ingrezza growth…strong asset durability (Ingrezza has exclusivity until 2038) …the launch of Crenessity, with U.S. sales potential of $800M-$1B in a congenital adrenal hyperplasia space that is bereft of non-steroidal options…and optionality on the pipeline at current levels.”

Soleno Therapeutics (SLNO) 43.89% YTD

“SLNO is poised for a successful launch with an experienced rare disease commercial team, ongoing payer engagement, and the Soleno One platform to provide end-to-end support for patients with consensus revenue expectations of $37.1M in 2025 and $241.4M in 2026.”

Verona Pharma (VRNA) 22.07% YTD

“We model peak sales of ~$4B by 2038, where consensus has 1Q25 and 2025 revenue at $52.7M (we model ~$43.2M) and $313.5M (we model ~$278.8M), respectively… We believe if VRNA achieves >$300M in revenue in 2025 it would be positioned to breakeven on cashflow, where mgmt. has previously noted we must consider an operating cashflow runway of ~$250-300M for FY25.”

data.title: Robotaxi race: Waymo is off to a strong start in Austin and gaining traction in other markets
data.description:
Waymo (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) has impressed in Austin during its early launch of a robotaxi service. The autonomous driving company's robotaxi service in Austin attracted 20% of the rides offered by Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) during the last week of March, per a report from YipitData. The takeaway is that more Austin rideshare customers were willing to take the autonomous Uber (NYSE:UBER) option for a test drive than some analysts predicted.  

YipitData also found that Waymo has generated more than 80% more driverless rides in its operating zone in Austin in the first 27 days of offering the service than it did in the early days of its San Francisco launch. 

Weighing in on the Waymo development, A16z partner Alex Immerman noted that when Waymo launched in August 2023 in San Franciso, Uber (UBER) had 66% market share in San Francisco and Lyft (LYFT) had the other 34%. As of a check November, Waymo had snagged 22% market share to match Lyft (LYFT) in the overlapping San Francisco region, while Uber's (UBER) market share declined to 55%. 

"Anecdotally, Waymo's wait times are longer than Uber and Lyft because they don't have enough cars on the road. But they are close enough to that acceptable threshold, that their superior product (clean, nice cars, quiet drivers, etc) tips the riders in their direction. It's possible when Waymo puts more cars on the road and reduces wait times to be in line with Uber and Lyft, their share could climb even faster," highlighted Immerman.

Waymo's autonomous driving service is currently available in Phoenix, San Francisco, Los Angeles, Austin, and general Silicon Valley region that includes Mountain View, Palo Alto, Los Altos, and parts of Sunnyvale.

Waymo's future competitors in U.S. autonomous driving markets will be Amazon's (AMZN) Zoox (ZOOX) and Tesla (TSLA). Globally, Baidu (BIDU) , WeRide (WRD), and Pony AI (PONY) are out in front of other competitors. In general, analysts believe the U.S. and global robotaxi market will be big enough to support a few major players.

data.title: AMD leads chip stocks higher, largely, amid China tariff reports
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) led chip stocks higher on Friday after a report that China tariffs would exempt U.S. semiconductor companies that outsource manufacturing.

AI chipmakers Nvidia (NVDA) rose about 3%, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) jumped around 5%. In addition, Broadcom (AVGO) and Qualcomm (QCOM) climbed about 3%, each.

Earlier on Friday, it was reported that China's import tariffs on U.S. goods provide an exemption for U.S. semiconductor companies that outsource their manufacturing. Reportedly, U.S. companies such as Qualcomm (QCOM) and AMD, which outsource manufacturing to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), are exempted as China's regulators classify these chips as originating from Taiwan.

Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) jumped about 3%. However, several chipmakers saw red. Texas Instruments (TXN) declined about 6%, and Intel (INTC) fell around 4%, and Micron (MU), GlobalFoundries (GFS), Analog Devices (ADI), and Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) each fell nearly 2%. Marvell Technology (MRVL) dipped roughly 1%.

Meanwhile, chip equipment makers: Applied Materials (AMAT) soared around 4%, KLA (KLAC) and Dutch company ASML (ASML), each rose nearly 2%. Lam Research (LRCX) climbed about 1%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Morgan stanley projects continued strength in equities and wealth management growth amid M&A opportunities
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Morgan Stanley (MS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ted Pick reported $17.7 billion in revenue, $2.60 in EPS, and a 23% ROTCE for Q1 2025. The firm achieved a record quarter in equities with over $4 billion in revenue, supported by strong institutional securities performance. Wealth Management also delivered $94 billion in net new assets, increasing total client assets to $7.7 trillion.
  • Pick highlighted the firm's CET1 capital ratio of 15.3%, stressing financial strength and flexibility for supporting clients and shareholders. He emphasized the importance of the firm’s five consecutive "clean" quarters and its ability to navigate volatile market conditions.
  • CFO Sharon Yeshaya noted record revenues of $17.7 billion, driven by strong advice and capital intermediation. Institutional Securities achieved $9 billion in revenue, growing 28% year-over-year, with particular strength in equities and fixed income underwriting. Advisory revenues were $563 million, supported by higher completed deals and mid-size transaction growth.

Outlook

  • Management noted robust M&A pipelines, with activity driven by financial sponsors and mid-size deal growth. While some near-term deal activity faced delays due to market volatility, the underlying demand for strategic advice and capital raising remains strong.
  • Wealth Management is expected to benefit from continued advisor recruitment and growing client engagement across all channels, with a focus on fee-based flows and workplace stock plan transitions.
  • CFO Yeshaya forecasted stability in net interest income (NII) for Q2 2025, despite potential seasonal declines in deposit sweeps due to tax payments.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 revenue reached $17.7 billion, with EPS at $2.60. Institutional Securities posted $9 billion in revenue, led by equities ($4.1 billion) and fixed income underwriting ($677 million). Derivatives and prime brokerage also performed strongly amid volatile markets.
  • Wealth Management reported $7.3 billion in revenue, supported by $30 billion in fee-based flows and $94 billion in net new assets. Pre-tax profit reached $2 billion, with a margin of 27%.
  • Investment Management revenues grew to $1.6 billion, driven by higher AUM at $1.6 trillion and strong net inflows of $5.4 billion.

Q&A

  • Steven Chubak (Wolfe Research) asked about the durability of recent equities performance and potential headwinds. CEO Pick attributed the strength to investments in technology and client engagement across regions and products. He also warned that weaker economic sentiment could dampen activity but noted that current volumes remain strong.
  • Christian Bolu (Autonomous Research) inquired about risk management amid heightened trading volatility. Pick emphasized the firm's disciplined risk approach and client engagement, highlighting consistent market-making activity.
  • Devin Ryan (Citizens JMP) asked about expense management and severance charges. Pick clarified that workforce reductions were part of ongoing performance management and alignment with long-term goals, while investments continue in high-growth areas.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the sustainability of strong equities performance and the pace of M&A recovery. Questions reflected concerns about market volatility and its impact on transaction pipelines.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks but displayed caution when addressing uncertainties in M&A timing and macroeconomic volatility during the Q&A session.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased to $17.7 billion in Q1 2025 from $16.2 billion in Q4 2024, driven by record equities and strong institutional securities results. EPS rose from $2.22 to $2.60.
  • Wealth Management net new assets improved to $94 billion, compared to $57 billion in the prior quarter, supported by stronger fee-based flows and advisor recruitment.
  • Institutional Securities showed sequential growth in equities and fixed income underwriting revenues, while advisory revenues remained stable.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted risks from macroeconomic volatility, geopolitical uncertainty, and potential delays in M&A and IPO pipelines. CFO Yeshaya noted the firm’s proactive approach to managing credit provisions and maintaining strong capital positions.
  • Analysts raised concerns about prolonged uncertainty impacting transaction volumes and the pace of recovery in capital markets activity.

Final Takeaway

Morgan Stanley reported a record quarter, showcasing strength in equities, wealth management, and institutional securities. With robust pipelines in M&A and IPOs, the firm is well-positioned despite macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainties. Management remains focused on disciplined execution and leveraging investments in technology and talent to drive durable growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: EOG Resources raised to Buy equivalent at Scotiabank; Chevron, Occidental downgraded
data.description:
EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) +2.4% in Friday's trading as Scotiabank upgrades shares to Sector Outperform from Sector Perform with a $130 price target, citing reasonable valuation compared to other mega-cap E&Ps, even as the company remains one of the industry's leading operators, particularly in its core Permian region.

"We believe the market will be willing to pay a fair premium for companies with a fortress balance sheet in today's volatile and uncertain market, and EOG currently offers the strongest balance sheet within our coverage with a net debt to capital ratio of less than 2% at year-end 2024," analyst Paul Cheng writes.

EOG (NYSE:EOG) ranks among the lowest oil beta names among large-cap E&Ps with a higher-than-average North American gas exposure, and the company's asset mix is well positioned for today's market, the analyst says.

Cheng also downgrades Occidental Petroleum (OXY) and Chevron (CVX) to Sector Perform from Sector Outperform with respective $40 and $143 price targets.

The analyst says Occidental's (OXY) leveraged position is more problematic compared to peers due to its untimely acquisition of CrownRock, with net debt/capital at nearly 54% at year-end 2024 - the worst within Scotiabank's coverage universe - and Chevron (CVX) has a high likelihood of reducing its current buyback run rate from the recent annual pace of ~$17.5B to $10B in the coming quarters, while Exxon Mobil has a higher probability of maintaining the current payout pace.

data.title: Fed's Musalem warns of growing stagflation risks over near term
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem said there is potential for stagflation to take hold over the near term amid tariff developments, pointing to rising inflation pressures alongside mounting risks to employment and economic growth.

“Uncertainty about the net effects and timing of new trade, immigration, fiscal and regulatory policies on prices, employment and economic activity is high, and a scenario in which inflation rises while at the same time the labor market softens is a distinct possibility that must be considered,” Musalem said in remarks prepared for delivery at an Arkansas Bankers Association event.

As such, he added, now is the time for the Fed to stay vigilant and carefully monitor incoming data as it relates to the path of monetary policy, echoing many of his colleagues calling for a wait-and-see approach.

The St. Louis Fed chief repeated his emphasis on keeping inflation expectations anchored, warning that tariffs might drive sustained inflation pressures. At the same time, he sees the U.S. economy growing at a more moderate clip.

"I consider it appropriate to 'lean against' second-round effects with monetary policy, although discerning between underlying inflation and the direct, indirect and second-round effects of tariffs is likely to bechallenging in real time," he said.

Earlier on Friday, the University of Michigan's five-year implied consumer inflation climbed further to 4.4% from 4.1%. Other than that, most gauges of long-term inflation expectations remain anchored, Musalem noted.

"Ensuring that inflation expectations remain well anchored allows for a balanced approach to monetary policy with appropriate focus on the maximum employment side of the mandate," Musalem said.

data.title: Asana CEO acquires shares worth ~$6.72M
data.description:
• Enterprise software company Asana (NYSE:ASAN) said in a filing that CEO Dustin Moskovitz acquired shares worth ~$6.72M.
  • Moskovitz acquired class A shares in three tranches - 159,025 shares at a weighted average purchase price per share of $14.07; 65,975 shares at $14.64; and 225,000 shares at $15.64.

data.title: Bill Ackman criticizes heavy use of leverage in the markets
data.description:
Bill Ackman, the billionaire investor and founder of Pershing Square Capital Management, questioned the use of highly leveraged ETFs and zero-day stock options, questioning their contribution to society and the economy.

“How have 3X leveraged ETFs and Zero Days to Expiration (0DTE) options advanced society or contributed to our economy?” he posted on X.com.

Leveraged funds allow investors to magnify their exposure to an asset or index. These funds are typically meant for short-term trading.

The ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF (NASDAQ:TQQQ), for example, is up more than $2.3B this week so far, and is on track for the biggest weekly inflow in its history.

The zero-day stock options deal with 24-hour contracts, and some investors say they heighten volatility in the equity markets.

The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY), Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), the S&P Index (SPX), and the S&P 500 Mini-SPX Index (XSP) list daily options.

“Investors and market commentators are reading too much into short-term market movements in rates, currencies and equities,” Ackman posted on X.com.

“I believe that it is much more likely that recent sharp moves in these asset classes is due to highly leveraged market participants being forced out of positions than due to fundamentals,” he said. “In other words, technical factors are driving the dramatic market moves.”

This has resulted in markets becoming highly unreliable as short-term impact indicators of policy changes, he said.

“I don’t understand how it is good for the world to allow investors in equities to operate with 10-1 leverage and investors in Treasurys and currencies to operate with 100-1 leverage,” he added. “When did we decide as a society to abandon the margin rules that were put in place to protect markets from this kind of volatility?”

Here are some of the top 3x leveraged ETFs, aside from TQQQ:

  • Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3x Shares (SOXL) – Average volume: 93M
  • Direxion Daily 20+ Year Treasury Bull 3X Shares (TMF) – Average volume: 139M
  • Direxion Daily S&P 500 Bull 3X Shares (SPXL) – Average volume: 5.16M
  • ProShares UltraPro S&P500 (UPRO) – Average volume: 6.5M
  • Direxion Daily Technology Bull 3X Shares (TECL) – Average volume: 1.9M
  • Direxion Daily Financial Bull 3X Shares (FAS) – Average volume: 887.6k
  • Direxion Daily Small Cap Bull 3X Shares (TNA) – Average volume: 15M
